Velocity Design Systems

Empowering teams to build intuitive, scalable digital experiences through collaborative design infrastructure.

Now Hiring: Staff UX Designer

San Francisco, CA (Hybrid) · Full-time · $220,000 - $310,000 annually

J

oin Velocity Design Systems as a Staff UX Designer to lead design strategy, architecture, and mentorship

across our platform. You will drive product direction on core features impacting millions of end-users,

establish design system standards, and lead cross-functional initiatives with engineering, product management,

and design teams. This role requires 8+ years of UX design experience with proven technical leadership, deep

expertise in design systems, and a track record of scaling design practices across large organizations.

A B O U T U S

Velocity Design Systems is a SaaS-focused design infrastructure company that helps enterprise teams scale their

design operations. We provide design system tools, component libraries, and design-to-development workflows

for mid-market and enterprise clients. With 120+ customers across fintech, healthcare, and SaaS industries, we're

passionate about making design leadership accessible and efficient.

Y O U R R O L E

Define and evolve the UX strategy for our core design platform, balancing user research insights with business

objectives and technical constraints across enterprise and SMB segments.

Lead the design of our component library and design system documentation using Figma, ensuring consistency,

accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA), and developer adoption across 3+ product teams.

Conduct foundational user research studies (qualitative and quantitative) to inform product decisions, using

tools like Maze and Hotjar to validate design hypotheses with 50+ enterprise customers quarterly.

Architect high-fidelity prototypes and interactive flows in Figma and Penpot, demonstrating complex design
interactions to stakeholders and enabling rapid iteration cycles.

Mentor 4-6 mid-level and junior designers, establishing design critique frameworks, code review processes for

design, and leveling up technical UX skills across the design organization.

Partner with engineering leads to establish design-to-code handoff standards using design tokens and

component specifications, reducing implementation time by 30%+ and ensuring accessibility standards.

Drive adoption of accessibility best practices (WCAG 2.1, color contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader

testing) across all product surfaces and establish internal a11y auditing processes.

Lead cross-functional design discovery for 2-3 major initiatives per year, owning research synthesis, design

requirements, and handoff to engineering stakeholders.

 Represent design at senior leadership forums, communicating UX impact on product roadmap, customer

acquisition, and retention metrics to executive stakeholders.

R E Q U I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

8+ years of professional UX design experience, with at least 3 years in a staff, principal, or senior leadership
design role.

Mastery of Figma with advanced skills in design systems, component architecture, and design token

management; proven ability to design and maintain scalable component libraries.

Deep expertise in accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1) and hands-on experience testing with assistive

technologies (screen readers, keyboard navigation, magnification tools).

Proven technical literacy: understanding of web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript fundamentals), design-

to-code workflows, and collaboration with engineering teams.

Strong portfolio demonstrating design system work, complex product design challenges, and measurable

business impact; experience designing B2B SaaS or design tools is a plus.

Demonstrated mentorship and people leadership skills, with examples of growing design talent and scaling
design practices within organizations.

D E S I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

Experience with design research methodologies (user interviews, usability testing, analytics) and tools such as

Maze, Hotjar, or Figma Slides for research synthesis.

Proficiency with prototyping tools beyond Figma such as Penpot or interactive prototyping frameworks

(Framer, Protopie).

Knowledge of design-to-developer handoff best practices, design tokens (using tools like Tokens Studio), and
component specifications.

Prior experience designing tools or infrastructure products for design/developer audiences.

T O O L S  O F  T H E T R A D E

Figma (design, prototyping, design systems, components), WCAG 2.1 & accessibility standards, Maze (user

research & testing), Hotjar (analytics & heatmaps), Penpot (open-source design alternative), Design tokens &

Tokens Studio, HTML, CSS, JavaScript fundamentals, Figma Dev Mode (design-to-code)

W E O F F E R

Competitive salary with equity (stock options) and annual performance bonuses

Comprehensive health coverage: medical, dental, vision, and mental wellness support

Flexible work-from-home policy with company stipend for home office setup

 Professional development budget ($3,000/year) for conferences, certifications, and courses

Generous paid time off: 4 weeks PTO + 10 company holidays + unlimited sick days

Design tool subscriptions (Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud, Penpot) and design hardware budget

Parental leave (16 weeks) and adoption assistance

401(k) matching (up to 5%) and HSA contributions

T O A P P LY

Submit your resume, cover letter, and a link to your portfolio (Figma file or case studies) to

careers@velocitydesignsystems.com. In your cover letter, please share an example of a design system or

component library you've led and its business impact. We review applications on a rolling basis and will follow up
within 2 weeks. Finalists will participate in a design leadership conversation, portfolio deep-dive, and cross-

functional panel interview.
company: making design leadership accessible and efficient
